LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT JEFFREY GOODMAN MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS ESTABLISHED THAT FACTS ESSENTIAL TO JUSTIFY OPPOSITION TO THE MOTION EXIST, BUT PLAINTIFF HAS BEEN UNABLE TO RECOVER THOSE FACTS IN DISCOVERY. (CCP SEC. 437C(H).) PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL STATES UNDER PENALTY OF PERJURY THAT COMPLETION OF DEFENDANT'S DEPOSITION AND RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES "ARE ESSENTIAL TO OPPOSING THE MOTION SINCE THEY GO TO THE HEART OF BREACH OF CONTRACT CLAIMS AS WELL AS MISREPRESENTATION AND BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTY AND DUTIES OF LOYALTY CLAIMS." (DECLARATION OF FARYAN ANDREW AFIFI, PAR. 9.) ADDITIONALLY, MR. AFIFI DECLARES THAT HE HAS MADE REASONABLE EFFORTS TO OBTAIN THE RELEVANT DISCOVERY, BUT HAS BEEN UNABLE TO DO SO BECAUSE OF DEFENDANT'S UNREASONABLE DELAYS. (ID.) DEFENDANT DOES NOT RESPOND TO PLAINTIFF'S ARGUMENTS ABOUT DISCOVERY IN REPLY, EXCEPT TO STATE THAT DISCOVERY IS NOW CLOSED. THIS ARGUMENT IS UNAVAILING, AS DENIAL OF THIS MOTION WILL PROVIDE PLAINTIFF THE OPPORTUNITY TO ELICIT TESTIMONY AT TRIAL. ADDITIONALLY, THE MOTION IS DENIED ON PROCEDURAL GROUNDS. DEFENDANT DID NOT COMPLY WITH CALIFORNIA RULE OF COURT 3.1350(I), WHICH PROVIDES THAT "[O]N REQUEST, A PARTY MUST WITHIN THREE DAYS PROVIDE TO THE OTHER PARTY'AN ELECTRONIC VERSION OF ITS SEPARATE STATEMENT." (EMPHASIS ADDED.) THE COURT HAS DISCRETION TO DENY A SUMMARY ADJUDICATION MOTION FOR FAILING TO COMPLY WITH THE CALIFORNIA RULES OF COURT. (TRUONG V. GLASSER (2009) 181 CAL.APP.4TH 102, 118.) GIVEN THE CLOSE PROXIMITY OF THIS MOTION TO TRIAL AND THE FACT THAT PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSITION WAS DUE NEAR THE CLOSE OF DISCOVERY WHEN THE PARTIES HAD TO PREPARE FOR MULTIPLE DEPOSITIONS, DEFENDANT PLACED AN UNNECESSARY BURDEN ON PLAINTIFF BY NOT FOLLOWING THE RULES OF COURT AND REQUIRING DEFENDANT TO RE-CREATE PLAINTIFF'S SEPARATE STATEMENT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER #5976.

LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS-DEFENDANTS OMNIMAGNET, LLC'S AND BOAZ MILGATER'S MOTION TO TRANSFER VENUE TO LOS ANGELES COUNTY IS DENIED. CROSS-COMPLAINANT GOODMAN'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS GRANTED. THE COURT IN LOS ANGELES HAS ALREADY RULED - ON ESSENTIALLY THE SAME MOTION FILED IN THIS COURT - THAT VENUE WAS NOT PROPER IN LOS ANGELES AND WAS PROPER IN SAN FRANCISCO. THE NEWLY CITED CASE OF BATTAGLIA ENTERPRISES, INC. V. SUPERIOR COURT OF SAN DIEGO CNTY., 215 CAL. APP. 4TH 309, 317 (2013), AS MODIFIED (APR. 29, 2013) IS OF NO EFFECT HERE BECAUSE CROSS-DEFENDANTS CANNOT ESTABLISH THAT THE VENUE SELECTION CLAUSES IN THE PARTIES' OPERATING AND EMPLOYMENT AGREEMENTS WOULD NOT "DISRUPT STATUTORY VENUE PROVISIONS." (ID.) THERE IS NO STATUTORY BASIS FOR VENUE IN LOS ANGELES. GOODMAN HAS ONCE AGAIN PRODUCED EVIDENCE THAT VENUE IS PROPER IN SAN FRANCISCO. MOVING PARTIES HAVE NOT SHOWN THAT THE CONVENIENCE OF THE WITNESSES JUSTIFIES TRANSFER. FINALLY, THE FILING OF THE COMPULSORY CROSS-COMPLAINT AGAINST OMNIMAGNET, LLC AND MIGALTER IS NOT A BASIS TO TRANSFER VENUE TO LOS ANGELES. THE CROSS-COMPLAINT IS TO BE TRIED IN THE SAME COUNTY (SAN FRANCISCO) THAT HAS BEEN ESTABLISHED AS THE PROPER VENUE FOR THE ORIGINAL COMPLAINT. (K.R.L. PARTNERSHIP V. SUPERIOR COURT (2004) 120 CAL. APP. 4TH 490, 506.) GOODMAN'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 =302/MJM

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ; Evidence
Off Calendar Jun-09-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Faryan Afifi, Esq., 310.407.3000, for plaintiff Omnimagnet.
Peter Mallon, Esq., 415.288.9800, for defendant Jeffrey Goodman.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT JEFFREY GOODMAN MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is denied. Plaintiff has established that facts essential to justify opposition to the motion exist, but Plaintiff has been unable to recover those facts in discovery. (CCP sec. 437c(h).) Plaintiff's counsel states under penalty of perjury that completion of Defendant's deposition and responses to special interrogatories "are essential to opposing the motion since they go to the heart of breach of contract claims as well as misrepresentation and breach of fiduciary duty and duties of loyalty claims." (Declaration of Faryan Andrew Afifi, par. 9.) Additionally, Mr. Afifi declares that he has made reasonable efforts to obtain the relevant discovery, but has been unable to do so because of Defendant's unreasonable delays. (Id.) Defendant does not respond to Plaintiff's arguments about discovery in reply, except to state that discovery is now closed. This argument is unavailing, as denial of this motion will provide Plaintiff the opportunity to elicit testimony at trial. Additionally, the motion is denied on procedural grounds. Defendant did not comply with California Rule of Court 3.1350(i), which provides that "[o]n request, a party MUST within three days provide to the other party'an electronic version of its separate statement." (Emphasis added.) The Court has discretion to deny a summary adjudication motion for failing to comply with the California Rules of Court. (Truong v. Glasser (2009) 181 Cal.App.4th 102, 118.) Given the close proximity of this motion to trial and the fact that Plaintiff's opposition was due near the close of discovery when the parties had to prepare for multiple depositions, Defendant placed an unnecessary burden on Plaintiff by not following the Rules of Court and requiring Defendant to re-create Plaintiff's separate statement. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.

Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Deft To Compel Pltf'S Further Responses To Form Interrogatories; Request For Admissions, Special Interrogatories, And Demand To Indentify And Produce Documents, And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Pltf
Off Calendar Nov-20-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Peter Mallon, Esq. of Hassard Bonnington LLP, Tel. 415.288.9800, for defendant/cross complainant and moving party;
Faryan Andrew Afifi, Esq. of the Afifi Law Group, Tel. 310.407.3000, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling which was not contested. CROSS COMPLAINANT JEFFREY GOODMAN's Motion To Compel Plaintiff's Further Responses To Form Interrogatories; Request For Admissions, Special Interrogatories, And Demand To Identify And Produce Documents, And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff is GRANTED. Defendant Jeffrey Goodman's motion is timely. Plaintiff OmniMagnet filed a special motion to strike under CCP Section 425.16 on June 11, 2013 - the 15th day after serving its responses. Movant Goodman filed his motion 10 days after entry of the court's September 30, 20133 order on the special motion to strike. Excluding the 111 days when all discovery proceedings were stayed pursuant to CCP Section 425.16(g), Defendant filed his motion within the 45-day time periods set forth in CCP Sections 2030.300(c), 2031.310(c), and 2033.290(c). Omnimagnet's assertion of blanket general objections and refusal to provide any substantive responses to even a single interrogatory, request for production, or request for admission constitutes a misuse of the discovery process under CCP Section 2023.030(a) and is without substantial justification. However, the court finds that imposition of sanctions against Omnimagnet would be unjust in light of Defendant Goodman's refusal to consent to entry of a stipulated protective order governing the treatment of information that a party designates for protection. Nevertheless, the court finds that Defendant Goodman complied with his obligation to meet and confer pursuant to CCP Section 2023.020. His attorney's June 6, 2013 meet and confer letter constituted a good-faith attempt to resolve informally the substantive discovery dispute. Within 30 days Plaintiff Omnimagnet shall serve verified code-compliant responses to Defendant Goodman's Form Interrogatories; Requests for Admissions; Special Interrogatories; and Demand to Identify and Produce Documents, and shall produce responsive documents to Defendant Goodman. Order signed in open court. Judge Pro Tem: Ernst Halperin; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.

Cross-Defendants Omnimagnet, Llc, A Delaware Limited Liability Motion To Transfer Venue
Off Calendar Aug-16-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. FARYAN ANDREW AFIFI, TEL. NO. (310) 407-3000 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. PETER MALLON OF HASSARD BONNINGTON LLP, TEL. NO. 288-9800 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Cross-Defendants Omnimagnet, LLC'S AND BOAZ MILGATER'S motion to transfer venue to Los Angeles County is denied. Cross-Complainant Goodman's request for judicial notice is granted. The Court in Los Angeles has already ruled - on essentially the same motion filed in this court - that venue was not proper in Los Angeles and was proper in San Francisco. The newly cited case of Battaglia Enterprises, Inc. v. Superior Court of San Diego Cnty., 215 Cal. App. 4th 309, 317 (2013), as modified (Apr. 29, 2013) is of no effect here because Cross-Defendants cannot establish that the venue selection clauses in the parties' Operating and Employment Agreements would not "disrupt statutory venue provisions." (Id.) There is no statutory basis for venue in Los Angeles. Goodman has once again produced evidence that venue is proper in San Francisco. Moving parties have not shown that the convenience of the witnesses justifies transfer. Finally, the filing of the compulsory cross-complaint against Omnimagnet, LLC and Migalter is not a basis to transfer venue to Los Angeles. The Cross-Complaint is to be tried in the same county (San Francisco) that has been established as the proper venue for the original complaint. (K.R.L. Partnership v. Superior Court (2004) 120 Cal. App. 4th 490, 506.) Goodman's request for sanctions is denied. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =302/MJM
